display_name: ADMISSION-EXEMPT LIQ (2026-07-17, AxiS: 'we need more buy volume on sol... we cant make money in memes with 2 buys a day'). The VOLUME-FIRST test of the validated admission edge. Root cause of admission_gated_v1/hivol starving (3 and 13 trades in 2.5 DAYS): they are NOT entry_stack_exempt, so the legacy fleet filter stack throttles candidates before the validated gate ever sees them. This racer goes stack-exempt (pump_dip pattern): the ONLY selector is the held-out-validated admission gate, liq>=34k (NG 59->49, win 21->25 on the raw firehose), + the 3-filter safety net + macro entry block. Entry-exit COHERENCE: sol_macro block (-0.3/-0.7) matches the (turn-fixed) sol_bail so it never buys the dump that the bail would punish — the 07-17 churn class (98 buys/10 tokens/84 bails at 5s, -$167/3.7h) is structurally excluded. A/B: vs admission_x_liqdemand (quality arm) and the stack-gated v1/hivol (control). Grade n>=30 closes: NG-rate, win-rate, $/entry, buys/day. PAPER $20.
